title: Patchouli and bergamot linen spray
subtitle: A fabric-safe room spray for bed linen and soft furnishings
excerpt: A lightly scented linen spray made with isopropyl alcohol, distilled water, patchouli, and bergamot essential oils. Spray onto bed linen, pillowcases, or curtains for a warm, earthy-citrus fragrance. The alcohol evaporates quickly and leaves no damp mark on fabric.
sourceNotes: Linen spray formulation: IPA-based carrier for quick evaporation on fabric. Total essential oil content at 3% for fabric-contact use (lower than room sprays that do not contact skin or fabric directly). Patchouli essential oil flash point above 90°C; bergamot above 60°C. Both safe for this application. Note: use furanocoumarin-free (FCF) bergamot in any product used near skin to avoid photosensitisation.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 spray bottle (~100 ml, approximately 60 to 80 sprays)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep total essential oil at 3% of formula for fabric-contact use.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the spray bottle away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. Patchouli is one of the most stable essential oils and holds well; the bergamot note fades faster. Shake before each use.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which an essential oil or carrier can ignite near a flame. Patchouli has a flash point above 90°C; bergamot above 60°C. The isopropyl alcohol in this spray is more flammable than the essential oils.

content.text: Patchouli lingers in fabric longer than most essential oils. With bergamot, the blend is warm and slightly citrusy with an earthy base. The 3% total essential oil concentration is deliberate for a fabric-contact spray: it gives a subtle scent and reduces the risk of oil staining on pale fabric. Use furanocoumarin-free (FCF) bergamot if the spray will contact skin directly, since standard bergamot contains bergapten, a photosensitiser. Both oils have
values safely above the isopropyl alcohol carrier; keep the spray away from naked flames.

items.prepNote: 60% of formula; 70% or 99% IPA; dissolves the essential oils before water is added
2% of formula; earthy, persistent base note
1% of formula; bright citrus lift; FCF bergamot preferred for fabric-contact use

content.text: Add both essential oils to the spray bottle first. Add the isopropyl alcohol and shake to dissolve the oils in the alcohol. Add the distilled water and shake again. Label with the date. Shake before each use.

Shake before use. Hold the bottle 30 to 40 cm from the fabric and spray 2 to 3 times over the surface. The alcohol evaporates within a few seconds, leaving the fragrance in the fabric. Do not over-apply; the patchouli note builds quickly and can become heavy on close fabric contact. Allow to dry fully before using the bed linen. Test on a hidden seam first on pale or delicate fabrics.
Store away from heat and light. Keeps 12 months. Patchouli is one of the most stable essential oils and the base note persists well; the bergamot note fades over months.
